notes: Solid general git conflict advice but lacks parallel-AI-agent-session framing — treats this as standard multi-developer workflow
Strong +73% pass rate delta in iteration 2 — the redesigned assertions successfully discriminate skill value.

Skill uniquely provides: structured agent briefing template (path+branch+scope+negative-scope), draft PR workflow for in-progress sessions, rebase-before-push discipline, --force-with-lease for safe force pushes, complete cleanup sequence including git worktree prune, and PRs-as-coordination-channel framing.
Baseline passes on: git worktree add mechanics, conventional commits title format, basic numbered option ranking — things Claude knows natively.

Key insight: the skill's differentiation is agent coordination abstraction, not git mechanics. Baseline treats these as standard developer workflows; skill treats them as parallel AI agent sessions requiring explicit scoping and coordination patterns.
With-skill runs use ~7800 more tokens on average and take ~23s longer — cost of reading skill + reference files. Worthwhile given the qualitative depth added.
Iteration 1 assertions were non-discriminating (100% both ways) because they tested git knowledge Claude already has. Iteration 2 assertions test agent-workflow-specific patterns.
